focus: TaylorMade Stealth slicing, can't hit driver syndrome, range vs course disconnect, OEM driver problems

PAIN.quote: After 3 sessions at the range, I can't stop the Stealth from going right, and I mean a long way right. Getting great distance - around 220m carry, but just can't get it to go straight
PAIN.source: GolfWRX - New Stealth driver- can't stop it slicing
PAIN.context: Brand new TaylorMade Stealth still slices

PAIN.quote: I used to never slice and hit very straight with the occasional bad shot going right... Probably should have paid the $'s and got fitted properly
PAIN.source: GolfWRX - New Stealth driver- can't stop it slicing
PAIN.context: Old driver worked, new one slices - regret no fitting

PAIN.quote: 9 times out of 10 when I hit my driver the ball viciously slices at almost a horizontal angle (comes off the tee straight, then does like a 90 degree turn right). This causes mayhem when I go to the course
PAIN.source: TheSandTrap - Can't fix my driver slice, any advice?

PAIN.quote: I have 'this one will be the good shot' syndrome and end up putting balls in the forest or like 3 holes over and end up taking drops that kill my score
PAIN.source: TheSandTrap - Can't fix my driver slice, any advice?

PAIN.quote: Lately I've just been keeping my driver in the bag, but I hate to feel like I'm giving up on it and I know that I have the potential to hit it far and straight, it's just killing my score lately
PAIN.source: TheSandTrap - Can't fix my driver slice, any advice?
PAIN.context: Benching driver feels like giving up

PAIN.quote: A few years ago I was hitting my driver consistently high 290's to occasionally over 340yds. I now find myself unable to hit it at all on the course but at the driving range I clear the fence which is 275 consistently but when I get on the course I can't hit it without a hook or slice
PAIN.source: TheSandTrap - Can't hit my driver to save my life!

PAIN.quote: considering I spent well over $200 for it I'd like to get use out of it! Obviously you can't see my swing here but any suggestions or people with similar issues?
PAIN.source: TheSandTrap - Can't hit my driver to save my life!

PAIN.quote: I'll have a few good shots and then get cocky and on the next tee I pull out the driver and sort of think, 'well what could really go wrong?' and then a whole lot goes wrong and my score goes in the gutter
PAIN.source: TheSandTrap - Can't fix my driver slice, any advice?

HOPE.quote: I stopped slicing by really focusing on an inside out swing. I did this by bringing the club down very slowly and making sure the end of the grip pointed at the ball. Not saying this will work, but it helped me get the feeling of coming at the ball from inside
HOPE.source: TheSandTrap - Can't fix my driver slice, any advice?

HOPE.quote: if you grip down a half inch, grip down an inch... if you grip down an inch, grip down an inch and a half, etc... you'll gain control
HOPE.source: TheSandTrap - Can't hit my driver to save my life!

HOPE.quote: maybe you just need to slow your swing down to where you got control and then increase power lil by lil till you start straying again. Even the pros are not straight with their power more than 60% of the time
HOPE.source: TheSandTrap - Can't hit my driver to save my life!

HOPE.quote: I've been teeing off with my hybrids which I don't hit very far but have a far less chance of slicing or doing something that'll force me to drop or end up in a terrible lie
HOPE.source: TheSandTrap - Can't fix my driver slice, any advice?

ROOT_CAUSE.quote: You're leaving the club face open. You're welcome
ROOT_CAUSE.source: GolfWRX - New Stealth driver- can't stop it slicing

ROOT_CAUSE.quote: Do you have any idea how long your old driver was? New drivers come 45.5 to 45.75 inches. If your old driver was 45 inches that would explain it bc it's long it takes more time to come square back up
ROOT_CAUSE.source: GolfWRX - New Stealth driver- can't stop it slicing
ROOT_CAUSE.context: New drivers longer = harder to square

ROOT_CAUSE.quote: The slice is being produced by coming across the ball from outside->in (over the top). Work on feeling like your right shoulder is low at impact and you are swinging for right field
ROOT_CAUSE.source: TheSandTrap - Can't fix my driver slice, any advice?

ROOT_CAUSE.quote: I've heard that a lot of people get tense and don't finish their backswing when they get nervous which can lead to a whole mess of problems
ROOT_CAUSE.source: TheSandTrap - Can't hit my driver to save my life!

ROOT_CAUSE.quote: The main difference I can see in the shafts is the Torque of the old Speeder is 6, whilst the TM Ventus Red 5 R is a 4.1. Not sure if that makes a difference
ROOT_CAUSE.source: GolfWRX - New Stealth driver- can't stop it slicing
ROOT_CAUSE.context: Lower torque shaft = harder to square face

SOLUTIONS_TRIED.quote: Tried everything I can think off, but thinking that the new shaft is a bit stiffer and for the last 8 years I have been adjusting my swing for a softer shaft
SOLUTIONS_TRIED.source: GolfWRX - New Stealth driver- can't stop it slicing

SOLUTIONS_TRIED.quote: I've tried different tee heights but It seems like even 'tee it high and let it fly' doesn't help me
SOLUTIONS_TRIED.source: TheSandTrap - Can't fix my driver slice, any advice?
SOLUTIONS_TRIED.context: Tee height changes don't fix slice

SOLUTIONS_TRIED.quote: I practice hitting balls off the fairway or grass with my driver and when you can take the club back and swing thru and hit the ball consistent with the driver off the deck you have found the ideal back swing for your driver
SOLUTIONS_TRIED.source: TheSandTrap - Can't hit my driver to save my life!

SOLUTIONS_TRIED.quote: Unlike many other golfers, I never took a lesson and kind of regret it. That should definitely be on your list of things to do in developing your swing. But, if you can't justify the cost, start with these mental images
SOLUTIONS_TRIED.source: TheSandTrap - Can't fix my driver slice, any advice?

COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.quote: If the OP is the type of person who doesn't want to take the time and effort to work on his swing and wants to buy a game, he should consider this
COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.source: GolfWRX - New Stealth driver- can't stop it slicing
COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.context: Draw bias driver suggested for those who won't fix swing

COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.quote: Should I send it back and get the D model maybe?
COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.source: GolfWRX - New Stealth driver- can't stop it slicing
COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.context: Considering draw model after standard fails

COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.quote: I'm right handed and am using a 10.5 degree callaway big bertha. I've tried different tee heights but It seems like even 'tee it high and let it fly' doesn't help me
COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.source: TheSandTrap - Can't fix my driver slice, any advice?

VILLAIN.quote: A 90 mph swing slicing the ball and still carrying it 240. Guess I have to get me one
VILLAIN.source: GolfWRX - New Stealth driver- can't stop it slicing
VILLAIN.context: Sarcastic response to inflated distance claims

VILLAIN.quote: I don't wanna sound mean or change subject but you hit your 4 iron 240 and you're a 20 handicap?
VILLAIN.source: TheSandTrap - Can't hit my driver to save my life!

VILLAIN.quote: Long hitters aren't that uncommon. There's more than a few guys at the range I go to that average over 300 yds. The difference between them and the PGA pros is...the pros are way more accurate
VILLAIN.source: TheSandTrap - Can't hit my driver to save my life!

VILLAIN.quote: you're more worried about what you can do with your driver, than what your driver can do for you
VILLAIN.source: TheSandTrap - Can't hit my driver to save my life!

sources_covered: GolfWRX - New Stealth driver- can't stop it slicing
TheSandTrap - Can't fix my driver slice, any advice?
TheSandTrap - Can't hit my driver to save my life!
high_value_themes_identified: Brand new TaylorMade Stealth still slices
New drivers longer = harder to square
